_meta.purpose: Grounded TCM tongue-reading knowledge base: per-feature (graded) meanings in TCM + plain language, and patterns aligned to the CCMQ 9-constitution model and WHO ICD-11 Ch.26 pattern names, with plain-language associated symptoms, follow-up questions (from validated CCMQ items), and specific traditional recommendations.
_meta.framing: Educational only. Every statement is 'traditionally associated with…', one framework's perspective — NOT a diagnosis. Plain-language glosses describe what practitioners in this tradition associate a sign with; they are not medical claims.
_meta.schema: features.<char>: graded meaning keyed by presence/value + points_to pattern weights. patterns.<id>: tcm_name (ICD-11-style), plain_name, explanation, associated_symptoms (plain), followup_questions (CCMQ-derived, with weights), recommendations.

overview: In TCM tongue reading, the tongue BODY (color, shape, cracks, tooth-marks) reflects Blood/Qi/Yang and the organs, while the COATING (color, thickness, greasiness) reflects digestion and factors such as Cold, Heat and Dampness. Signs are read by degree and in combination, never one at a time.
sources: Maciocia G., Tongue Diagnosis in Chinese Medicine & Foundations of Chinese Medicine
Kirschbaum B., Atlas of Chinese Tongue Diagnosis
WHO ICD-11 Chapter 26 (Traditional Medicine, Module 1) — standardized pattern names
Wang Qi (Beijing Univ. of Chinese Medicine), Constitution in Chinese Medicine Questionnaire (CCMQ) — validated 9-constitution model & items
SymMap (Nucleic Acids Research 2019) — TCM symptom ↔ modern-medicine symptom mapping

coating.label: Coating thickness (greasiness)
coating.present_tcm: Dampness or Phlegm the digestion isn't transforming
coating.present_plain: sluggish digestion — a heavy, greasy or thick coating is traditionally linked to bloating, heaviness after meals, and feeling foggy
coating.absent_plain: a thin, non-greasy coating is considered the healthy baseline (good digestive function)

white.plain_gloss: a thin white coating is the normal, healthy coating; only a thick white coating leans 'cold/damp'

light_yellow.plain_gloss: the start of a 'warmer'/inflammatory tendency or mild damp-heat

yellow.plain_gloss: traditionally linked to internal 'heat' — think thirst, feeling warm, or richer diet; with a greasy texture, damp-heat

light.plain_gloss: a pale tongue is traditionally read as 'not enough Blood' — under-nourished; linked to tiredness, pallor, dizziness, and (in this tradition) a low-blood/low-iron tendency

regular.plain_gloss: a healthy, lively body color — balanced Qi and Blood

dark.tcm_term: red / dark body (Heat or Blood stasis)
dark.plain_gloss: a redder body is linked to 'heat' (feeling hot, restless); a dusky, purple-dark body to sluggish circulation

fissure.label: Fissures / cracks
fissure.present_tcm: depletion of Yin or Body Fluids, often with some Heat that 'dries' the surface
fissure.present_plain: traditionally linked to dryness — not enough rest, fluids, or 'cooling' reserves; think dry mouth or feeling run-down
fissure.absent_plain: a smooth body without cracks suggests fluids/Yin are well preserved

tooth_mk.label: Tooth marks (scalloped edges)
tooth_mk.present_tcm: a swollen tongue from Spleen-Qi (digestive) deficiency with Dampness
tooth_mk.present_plain: traditionally linked to low digestive energy and fluid retention — tiredness, bloating, puffiness
tooth_mk.absent_plain: smooth edges suggest the digestion is handling fluids well

peeled_coating.label: peeled / mirror coating
peeled_coating.present_tcm: Stomach / Yin depletion — the protective coating has worn away in patches
peeled_coating.present_plain: linked to dryness and depleted reserves — poor appetite, feeling run-down, dry mouth

red_tongue.label: red tongue body
red_tongue.present_plain: linked to 'heat' signs — feeling warm, thirst, irritability, or a rich/spicy diet

purple_body.label: purple / dusky body
purple_body.present_tcm: Blood stasis — obstructed or sluggish circulation
purple_body.present_plain: linked to sluggish circulation — fixed aches, cold extremities, dull complexion

swollen.label: swollen / enlarged body
swollen.present_tcm: Dampness and/or Qi deficiency (the tongue is puffy and retains fluid)
swollen.present_plain: linked to fluid retention and sluggish digestion — puffiness, heaviness, tiredness

thin.label: thin body
thin.present_plain: linked to being under-nourished — tiredness, dryness, poor recovery

red_dots.label: red dots / prickles
red_dots.present_tcm: Heat (often rising Heat), read by where the dots cluster
red_dots.present_plain: linked to 'heat' — stress, irritability, feeling warm, breakouts

black_coating.label: grey-black coating
black_coating.present_tcm: an extreme sign — severe Heat or severe Cold (interpret with the rest)
black_coating.present_plain: an unusual finding worth a professional look; can follow illness, smoking, or some foods

slippery_coating.label: wet / slippery coating
slippery_coating.present_plain: linked to fluid retention and sluggish digestion — heaviness, puffiness

spleen_qi_deficiency.explanation: In TCM the Spleen governs digestion and fluid metabolism. Tooth-marks + a pale body + a thin coating are the classic tongue picture of Spleen-qi deficiency — traditionally associated with tiredness and weak digestion.
associated_symptoms: (in this tradition, associated with low iron / anemia tendencies)
cold hands or a soft, quiet voice
spleen_qi_deficiency.modern_correlation: traditionally overlaps with functional digestive complaints and low-iron / fatigue presentations — educational only, not a diagnosis; a doctor can check iron/ferritin

followup_questions.q: Do you often feel bloated or overly full after eating?
Do you get tired easily, especially after meals?
Are your stools often loose or unformed?

diet: Favour warm, cooked, easy-to-digest meals (soups, stews, congee)
Reduce cold/raw foods, iced drinks, excess dairy and sugar
Eat at regular times; avoid overeating or eating late
lifestyle: Gentle regular movement (walking, qigong) rather than exhausting exercise
If tiredness/pallor persist, consider checking iron/ferritin with a doctor

phlegm_dampness.explanation: A thick, greasy coating (± tooth-marks, a swollen tongue) is the hallmark of Dampness/Phlegm — fluids not being transformed. Traditionally linked to heaviness and sluggish digestion.
associated_symptoms: a sticky or coated feeling in the mouth

followup_questions.q: Do you often feel your body or head is heavy, foggy, or not fresh?
Do you notice puffiness or water retention?
Do you often have phlegm or a coated feeling in the mouth?

diet: Reduce greasy, fried, sweet and cold/raw foods and dairy
Favour lighter, warm, well-cooked meals; add aromatic spices (ginger, cardamom)
Avoid overeating
lifestyle: Regular movement to help the body 'move' dampness
Keep warm and dry; avoid sitting in damp/cold environments

damp_heat.explanation: A yellow, greasy coating (often with a redder body) is the classic Damp-Heat picture — dampness combined with heat.
associated_symptoms: a bitter or sticky taste in the mouth
a general heaviness in the body

followup_questions.q: Do you often have a bitter or sticky taste in your mouth?
Do you tend to feel warm, flushed, or irritable?
Is your skin oily or prone to breakouts?

diet: Favour lighter, cooling foods (vegetables, mung bean, barley water)
Reduce alcohol, spicy, greasy, and very sweet foods
Stay hydrated with water rather than sugary drinks
lifestyle: Avoid overheating; keep a regular sleep schedule

yin_deficiency.explanation: Cracks in the body (especially with a red body and scanty coating) are traditionally read as Yin/fluid depletion with 'empty heat' — often from overwork, poor sleep, or dryness.
associated_symptoms: warm palms/soles or feeling hot in the evening
restless or poor sleep, night sweats

followup_questions.q: Do you often have a dry mouth or throat?
Do you get warm palms/soles, night sweats, or restless sleep?

diet: Favour moistening, nourishing foods (pear, cooked greens, soups, sesame)
Reduce coffee, alcohol, and very spicy/dry foods
Stay well hydrated

yang_deficiency.explanation: A pale, swollen, wet tongue with a white coating is traditionally linked to Yang deficiency — not enough internal 'warmth' to move fluids.
associated_symptoms: feeling cold easily; cold hands and feet
frequent urination or early-morning loose stools
cold or achy lower back and knees

followup_questions.q: Do you feel cold easily, or have cold hands and feet?
Do you strongly prefer warm food and drinks?

diet: Favour warm, cooked foods and warming spices (ginger, cinnamon)
Avoid cold/raw foods and iced drinks
lifestyle: Protect the lower back and abdomen from cold

blood_deficiency.plain_name: under-nourished (a 'low blood / low iron' tendency in this tradition)
blood_deficiency.explanation: A pale body (often with tooth-marks) is traditionally read as Blood deficiency — under-nourishment of the body and mind. In this tradition it overlaps with presentations that modern medicine would check for iron-deficiency/anemia.
blood_deficiency.modern_correlation: the pale-tongue picture traditionally overlaps with iron-deficiency / anaemia presentations (low haemoglobin) — educational only; consider a doctor checking a full blood count + ferritin

followup_questions.q: Do you often feel dizzy or lightheaded, especially when standing up?
Is your complexion pale, or your nails/hair brittle?
Do you often feel tired with poor concentration?

diet: Favour nourishing cooked foods: dark leafy greens, beets, dates, black sesame, good-quality protein
Pair iron-rich foods with vitamin C; reduce excess coffee/tea with meals
lifestyle: If fatigue/pallor/dizziness persist, consider a doctor checking iron/ferritin and full blood count

blood_stasis.explanation: A notably dark, dusky or purplish body is traditionally read as Blood stasis — circulation that has become sluggish or obstructed.
associated_symptoms: fixed, sharp or stabbing aches (same spot)

followup_questions.q: Do you get fixed, sharp aches that stay in one spot?
Are your hands and feet often cold?

diet: Favour foods traditionally used to 'move' blood (small amounts of ginger, turmeric, hawthorn)
Reduce heavy, greasy foods
lifestyle: Regular gentle movement and warmth to support circulation

balanced.explanation: A pale-red body with a thin coating, no cracks and no tooth-marks is the traditional picture of balanced Qi, Blood and Fluids with healthy digestion.

diet: Maintain a varied, balanced diet of mostly warm, cooked foods
lifestyle: Maintain regular sleep, movement and stress balance

qi_stagnation.explanation: Traditionally shown by slightly red or purple tongue SIDES (the Liver/Gallbladder zone) or a tense body. Often stress-related. (Our model reads global features, so this is mainly confirmed via the questions below.)
qi_stagnation.modern_correlation: overlaps with stress-related tension and functional digestive complaints (educational, not a diagnosis)

followup_questions.q: Do you often feel stressed, tense, or find yourself sighing a lot?
Does stress noticeably upset your digestion?

diet: Regular meals; go easy on caffeine and alcohol
Aromatic, gently 'moving' foods (mint, citrus peel, fennel)
lifestyle: Regular movement and stress-release (walking, stretching, breathing)

special_diathesis.explanation: A constitutional sensitivity with no single defining tongue sign — recognised mainly from history. Included so the traditional 9-constitution set is complete.
associated_symptoms: sensitivity to weather, pollen or certain medicines
special_diathesis.modern_correlation: overlaps with atopic / allergic tendencies (educational, not a diagnosis)

followup_questions.q: Do you have allergies, hay fever, or easily-reacting / sensitive skin?

diet: Notice and reduce personal trigger foods; keep meals simple and fresh
lifestyle: Identify environmental triggers; support steady sleep and stress balance

regions._note: Where a sign appears maps to an organ system (classical tongue zoning). Our model currently reads GLOBAL features; zoning is provided as educational context and is a roadmap detection target (TCM-Tongue organ classes + TonguExpert subregions). Source: Song Weijiang, Holistic Theory of Tongue Diagnosis; Maciocia.

tip.note: redness or red dots at the tip → Heart/Lung 'heat' (often stress, poor sleep)

center.note: a central crack or thick central coating → Stomach/Spleen (digestion)

sides.note: redness or purple at the sides → Liver/Gallbladder (tension, stress)

root.note: a thick coating at the root → lower-body dampness; a pale root → Kidney 'reserve'

combination_rules.note: pale + swollen + moist/thin-white = Spleen/Kidney Yang deficiency, NOT damp-heat
a wet/glossy tongue argues AGAINST heat/dryness (heat dries the surface)
greasy WHITE coat = cold-damp/phlegm, not damp-heat
a RED dark tongue is Heat; only PURPLE dark is stasis
red + cracks + little/peeled coat = empty-heat (Yin deficiency), not damp-heat
cracks on a THICK GREASY coat may be cracks in the COATING, not true body fissures — discount the Yin-deficiency reading that fissures would otherwise drive
